summary: Retested security hardening and regressions on public URL: JWT is now enforced for prompt/template writes, forged x-user-* headers do not bypass auth, and login-first builder + admin role management flows are working.
action_items: Optional cleanup: remove legacy x-user-role/x-user-name request header usage in frontend interceptor to reduce confusion, since backend now correctly uses JWT user context.
critical_code_review_comments: Security hardening in backend/server.py is correctly applied for /api/prompts and /api/templates writes: Authorization is required and role is enforced from JWT-derived current user.
frontend/src/lib/api.js still sends x-user-role and x-user-name for every request; not exploitable now, but redundant and can be removed for defense-in-depth clarity.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all requested login/activity/builder/admin-role flows passed in executed scenarios)

seed_data_creation: Created users: sec_editor_<uuid>, sec_viewer_<uuid>, test_ui_u<suffix>, rt_editor_403433. Created drafts via UI/API during regression validation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security regression coverage now includes forged-header attempts with no token, invalid token, viewer/editor token + forged admin headers. UI regression verified admin login->activity->builder save and Team Roles create-user flow; editor login can save draft and cannot access Team Roles nav.
rca of the issue: Previous vulnerability came from trusting client-provided x-user-role/x-user-name on protected write endpoints. Current build resolves this by validating Bearer JWT and deriving role/username server-side via get_current_user_from_authorization before enforce_role checks.
